name: Waist-Watching Western Omelets
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Red bell pepper, cored and cut into thin strips
1 medium Green bell pepper, cored and cut into thin strips
4 ounces Turkey ham, cut into julienne strips (about 1 cup)
Salt and black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, heat the oil.
2. Add red and green peppers and turkey ham; cook for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring frequently until peppers are crisp-tender.
3. Season lightly with salt and pepper; remove from heat and keep warm.
4. In a small bowl using a wire whisk or fork, beat 1 egg and 1 egg white until blended and frothy.
5. Spray a 6-inch nonstick skillet or omelet pan with nonstick vegetable spray; set over medium heat.
6. Pour the egg mixture into the skillet; using the flat side of a spatula, stir briskly in circular motion while shaking the pan back and forth over the heat.
7. When the liquid has just begun to set, stop stirring; let the omelet cook for 30 to 40 seconds longer until edges and bottom are set but the center is still soft.
8. Loosen the omelet around the edges with a spatula until it moves freely; remove from heat.
9. Arrange 1/4 of the reserved pepper mixture on the side of the omelet farthest from the skillet handle.
10. Tilt the handle of the skillet up; using a spatula, lift the edge of the omelet nearest the handle and over the peppers.
11. Slide the omelet onto a plate; garnish with parsley sprig if desired.
12. Serve immediately. Repeat to make three more omelets. Makes 4 servings.
